title: The chemical properties of the halogen elements and the hydrogen halides
summary: 9701 AS halogens and HX — oxidising power, bond enthalpy, acid strength, and displacement reactions.
learningObjectives: Describe trend in oxidising power of halogens.
Explain acid strength of HX(aq) down the group.
Account for anomalously high HF bond enthalpy.
Write equations for halogen displacement reactions.

simpleExplanation.title: Halogens as oxidising agents
simpleExplanation.summary: Oxidising power decreases F₂ → I₂. H–X bond strength falls HF → HI, but acid strength in water rises HF → HI due to bond breaking in aqueous ionisation.
steps: Cl₂ + 2Br⁻ → 2Cl⁻ + Br₂ — more reactive halogen displaces less reactive.
HF weak acid — strong H–F bond; HCl–HI fully ionise.
F₂ most electronegative — strongest oxidising agent.
Disproportionation of Cl₂ in alkali gives Cl⁻ and ClO⁻.

sections.content: **Halogen chemistry** (11.2–11.4) spans Paper 1 MCQ and Paper 2 mechanisms. Link **electronegativity** to **oxidising power** and **HX acid strength**.
